business.headline: Infosys, Tech Mahindra, TCS: Why are IT stocks gaining today?US rate-cut expectations and hopes of a rebound in technology spending are driving fresh interest in IT stocks, helping the sector outperform the broader market after months of lagging.

Sensex gains nearly 300 points, Nifty above 23,600; IndiGo jumps 5%The BSE Sensex was up 312.44 points, or 0.42%, at 75,088.18 at 9:20 am, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 86.20 points, or 0.37%, to 23,633.95.

Can a 10-year SIP really make you rich? Here's the truth you need to knowInvest a few thousand rupees every month, stay invested for 10 years, and become rich, that is the promise often attached to mutual fund SIPs. But is wealth creation really that simple, or is there more to the story?

Commercial LPG cylinders become costlier from today, Delhi price up by Rs 42Commercial LPG cylinder prices were raised in Delhi and Kolkata from June 1. The move came alongside the government's push for stronger reserves and tougher anti-hoarding checks. However, there has been no change in the price of domestic LPG cylinders used by households.

Indians are buying homes with money they haven't earned yetAs home prices race ahead of incomes, more Indians are stretching EMIs into retirement, cutting savings and relying on decades of future earnings to keep the dream of homeownership alive. This is the story of how India's housing dream is increasingly being financed by tomorrow's income.

Petrol, diesel, ATF export duty cut: What changes from June 1The government has cut export duties on petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel for the fortnight from June 1. The move follows its price-linked review mechanism, while domestic fuel excise and retail rates remain unchanged.

CNG prices hiked by Rs 2 per kg in Mumbai, Thane, Navi MumbaiThis is the second hike in less than a month, following an earlier revision on May 14. The cumulative Rs 4 rise is expected to impact daily commuters, taxi operators and commercial vehicle users who rely heavily on CNG.

Illness, insurance and the cost of timingWhat happens when a chronic illness arrives before health insurance? One Multiple Sclerosis patient's journey highlights the challenges of getting coverage for pre-existing diseases in India.

Will Kwality Wall's big dairy push help it compete better in India?The company is making changes across products, pricing, factories and supply systems. Around half of Kwality Wall's products are expected to be dairy-based this year, while most of the shift will be completed next year.

Could Delhi HC's Hindware verdict reshape Google Ads and digital marketing in India?The Delhi High Court has ruled that Google cannot allow rival companies to bid on the trademarked keyword "HINDWARE" through its advertising platform.

Sensex, Nifty crash over inflation concern on poor rain forecastThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,092.06 points, or 1.44%, to close at 74,775.74, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 359.40 points, or 1.50%, to settle at 23,547.75.

Absolut Vodka maker battles Delhi ban as Rs 3,000 crore tax dispute deepensThe Delhi High Court has rejected Pernod Ricard's plea to resume liquor sales in the capital. The order adds to a separate customs dispute that has put nearly Rs 3,000 crore at stake.

How Gen Z and women buyers are turning India into the 2nd-largest diamond marketWhat if diamonds were no longer saved for "special occasions"? Across India, Gen Z shoppers and self-buying women are increasingly choosing diamonds as everyday luxury, a shift that is helping the country emerge as the world's second-largest diamond market.

Are India's stock investors overtaxed? Vijay Kedia thinks so, offers 3 solutionsVeteran investor Vijay Kedia has urged the Finance Ministry that the country's tax policy should reward long-term wealth creation instead of burdening those who provide patient capital to businesses.

From rising prices to rupee pressure: 5 key takeaways from RBI's annual reportIndia's economy remains strong, but challenges are growing. Slower growth, rising inflation risks and geopolitical tensions may test the economy in the coming months.

A war away, but not far away: RBI's warning for India's economyFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Under-construction or ready-to-move home: Which saves me more money in the long run?Buying a home is exciting, but one question often leaves buyers confused: should you go for a cheaper under-construction property or pay more for a ready-to-move home? Let's have a look.

Inspection Raj in the service of employers evading complianceIn the ninth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how reduced scrutiny in the name of "ease of doing business" may create space for wilful labour law violations.

Why Delta Corp share price crashed 16% todayDelta Corp shares slid 16% after the Supreme Court upheld the government's retrospective 28 per cent GST levy on online gaming companies. The ruling has intensified concerns over large tax demands and compliance costs across the sector.

8th Pay Commission: Pensioners seek option to choose between OPS, NPS, UPSEmployee and pensioner groups have urged the 8th Pay Commission to let government staff choose between OPS, NPS and UPS. The demand has put retirement security and clarity over post-retirement income at the centre of the wider pay panel discussions.

entertainment.headline: Mahhi Vij's farewell note gets Jay Bhanushali's support Mahhi Vij shared an emotional farewell note as she signed off from Sehar Hone Ko Hai and Kausar. Jay Bhanushali's supportive comment drew notice as the post revisited their respectful equation after separation.

Log velle hai: Bobby Deol shuts down fallout rumours with Alia Bhatt on Alpha set Bobby Deol set the record straight and denied reports of a fallout with Alia Bhatt during the making of Alpha. Praising her work ethic, he said much of what circulates online is inaccurate.

WATCH: Poorna Jagannathan on South Asian Representation in the West, Fred Armisen, Deli Boys In an exclusive chat with India Today's Bhavna Agarwal, Jio Hotstar's Deli Boys stars Poorna Jagannathan and Fred Armisen discuss Season 2, their unexpected on-screen chemistry, cultural discoveries on set, and the evolution of South Asian representation in Western media.

BTS unveils FESTA 2026 timeline as all seven members reunite BTS has unveiled the full FESTA 2026 schedule for its anniversary celebrations with ARMY. The line-up marks a full-circle moment, pairing the seven-member reunion with new music, a revived variety show and the ARIRANG tour.

Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt's son Maddox seeks to drop 'Pitt' from surname Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt's eldest son, Maddox Jolie-Pitt, has reportedly filed to drop his father's surname, becoming the latest among the former couple's children to embrace the Jolie name.

Anushka-Virat dance it out at RCB after-party following 2nd IPL win. Viral video Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli were seen dancing at a special party after RCB's IPL title win. The video from the celebration captured the mood as the couple joined the victory festivities.

Why Vijay chose Jana Nayagan over Karuppu as his final film Director RJ Balaji, in a recent interview, said Karuppu was once discussed as Vijay's final film before politics. The choice of Jana Nayagan instead underlines how closely cinema and campaign messaging intersected.

Anushka Sharma's proud-wife moment after Virat guides RCB to 2nd IPL title Anushka Sharma cheered Virat Kohli as RCB defended their IPL title against Gujarat Titans. She gave a shout-out to her cricketer husband with a special post on social media.

Priyanka Chopra unwinds amid Varanasi shoot with a swim and self-care Priyanka Chopra shared glimpses of her Sunday break in Hyderabad while shooting SS Rajamouli's next Varanasi. The post offered fans a look at her downtime before she returned to work on the film.

Veteran singer Suman Kalyanpur dies at 89, leaving behind timeless melodies Veteran playback singer Suman Kalyanpur died on Sunday evening at her Mumbai residence, aged 89. Tributes said her songs across languages will continue to resonate for generations.

Murder Mindfully 2 review: Tom Shilling dark comedy is not as fresh, but still funRelease Date:Thu May 28, 2026 German dark comedy Murder Mindfully returns with more therapy, more trouble and more bodies. Season 2 keeps its dark humour intact, even when the story gets a little wild.

Japan was extra fun: Rashmika shares vacation pics, ft Vijay Deverakonda Rashmika Mandanna shared new photos and videos from her Japan trip after attending the Anime Awards in Tokyo. The post also featured candid glimpses of Vijay Deverakonda as the couple spent time together abroad.

Dua Lipa, Callum Turner get married in London. Viral pics take over the internet Dua Lipa and Callum Turner got married in an intimate ceremony in London in the presence of their loved ones. Photos from the ceremony, obtained by international organisations, are now doing the rounds on social media.

Emotional Ajith Kumar pays last respects to mother, thanks police for support Actor Ajith Kumar bid farewell to his mother Mohini Mani at a private cremation in Chennai and thanked police personnel for the funeral arrangements. The final rites drew condolences from the film fraternity as the family mourned the loss in private.

Pakistani journalist born in Lyari backs Dhurandhar: Everything shown was true Pakistani journalist Arif Aajakia defended Dhurandhar's portrayal of Karachi's Lyari, saying the film accurately reflects the area's reality. He recounted his own experience growing up in Lyari.

Shekhar Kapur wants to cast IPL sensation Vaibhav Suryavanshi in Masoom Shekhar Kapur said he could have cast IPL sensation Vaibhav Suryavanshi in Masoom if the teenager were not already a sensational cricketer.

Pranit More under fire for 'Peak Gurgaon content', Uorfi Javed reacts A viral stand-up clip showed comedian Pranit More laughing at an audience member's crude account of a date. The video drew sharp backlash online, with Uorfi Javed calling it disgusting and users questioning his reaction.

Ranveer Singh's love for Arsenal takes centre stage amid Don 3 row Actor Ranveer Singh shared a series of posts for Arsenal after the club's Champions League defeat and posted photos from Budapest. The actor's updates came even as the controversy around Don 3 continued to make buzz back home.

Madhuri Dixit defends Aishwarya Rai over Cannes trolling, praises her global legacy Madhuri Dixit has defended Aishwarya Rai after the latter was trolled over her Cannes appearance. She said such comments ignore achievement and send the wrong message to youngsters.

general news.headline: Rahul Gandhi flags 'phone-scanned' answer sheets as CBSE contract row deepensCongress leader Rahul Gandhi accused CBSE and its vendor of using mobile phones to scan Class 12 answer sheets after tender norms were changed. The charge has intensified scrutiny of the board's digital evaluation system, with students and researchers raising security and procurement concerns.

Day after attack on Abhishek, 60 TMC MLAs skip Mamata's legislators' meetingThe Trinamool Congress was forced to call off a legislators' meeting at Mamata Banerjee's residence after only 20 of the party's 80 newly elected MLAs attended.

PM Balen Shah's 'Nepal encroached India too' remark triggers backlash at homeNepal Prime Minister Balendra 'Balen' Shah told Parliament that India and Nepal had encroached on each other's territory. The remark drew opposition protests and renewed focus on the Lipulekh-Kalapani border dispute.

Delhi's Shubham Kumar tops JEE Advanced 2026; 56,880 candidates qualifyShubham Kumar of the IIT Delhi zone has topped JEE Advanced 2026, scoring 330 out of 360 marks. The results, announced on Sunday, saw 56,880 candidates qualify from among 1.79 lakh test-takers, while Arohi Deshpande emerged as the highest-ranked female candidate.

No gym, only khana? Why Delhi Gymkhana order is making elite clubs sweatOccupying some of India's most valuable, government-owned real estate, elite clubs from Delhi to Mumbai are facing uncomfortable questions. The Delhi Gymkhana Club row has sparked a conversation on whether public land should continue to support private privilege. The row has left the elites sweating.

Hours after wedding, Indian-origin pilot killed in chopper crash, bride survivesHours after saying "I do", Dave Fiji was killed in a helicopter crash in Georgia's Dawson County and his bride Jessni was injured. The accident has left his parents, who emigrated from Kerala, grieving.

Indians are buying homes with money they haven't earned yetAs home prices race ahead of incomes, more Indians are stretching EMIs into retirement, cutting savings and relying on decades of future earnings to keep the dream of homeownership alive. This is the story of how India's housing dream is increasingly being financed by tomorrow's income.

I took a year off while everyone moved forward: What I learnt along the wayA 31-year-old media professional recounts leaving work for a year after burnout. The break reshaped the writer's relationship with work, rest and ambition.

US, Iran trade fresh strikes near Strait of Hormuz over weekend amid ceasefireThe United States said it struck Iranian radar and drone control sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island over the weekend. The action, linked by Washington to the downing of a US MQ-1 drone, marked a fresh rise in tensions despite a ceasefire.

MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma, Rajat Patidar: The IPL's most unlikely trilogyIPL 2026 final: He came as a replacement player, went unsold at auction, and once postponed his own wedding for a tournament that had seemingly forgotten him. Two IPL titles later, Rajat Patidar has joined the most exclusive club in Indian cricket, entirely on his own terms.

The 20% Question: Re-evaluation returns, and CBSE faces its next big credibility testAs the CBSE Class 12 verification and re-evaluation process begins today, the board could face nearly 80,000 applications if Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's estimate proves accurate. For students, the issue is no longer just marks, but trust in the evaluation process.

Anushka-Virat dance it out at RCB after-party following 2nd IPL win. Viral videoAnushka Sharma and Virat Kohli were seen dancing at a special party after RCB's IPL title win. The video from the celebration captured the mood as the couple joined the victory festivities.

Did Iran's President really offer to quit? Tehran says 'wishful thinking'Masoud Pezeshkian haD reportedly sent a resignation letter to the Office of the Supreme Leader. The move pointed to a deepening split between Iran's elected government and hardline power centres.

Injustice to RCB fans: DK Shivakumar says IPL final should have been in BengaluruRCB won a second straight IPL title by beating Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad. DK Shivakumar said Bengaluru and its fans were wronged after missing the final.

Goodbye Western Disturbance: North India's rain party ends late Monday nightThe Western Disturbance providing relief from the extreme conditions in North India will exit the region by Monday night. Residents can expect lingering rainfall before it clears.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's namaste to Virat Kohli is IPL 2026's most wholesome video yetVaibhav Sooryavanshi greeted Virat Kohli with a namaste and shared a brief hug after the IPL 2026 final. The moment has gone viral.

Commercial LPG cylinders become costlier from today, Delhi price up by Rs 42Commercial LPG cylinder prices were raised in Delhi and Kolkata from June 1. The move came alongside the government's push for stronger reserves and tougher anti-hoarding checks. However, there has been no change in the price of domestic LPG cylinders used by households.

Holiday plan, job hope end under rubble: Delhi building collapse kills 2 friendsA multi-storey building on Western Marg in Delhi's Saidulajab area collapsed on Saturday evening, bringing down tonnes of debris onto a tin-shed canteen popular among students preparing for competitive examinations. Six people have been killed in the incident.

Ghaziabad teen murder accused killed in encounter, his father among 3 arrestedPolice had been searching for the main accused, Asad, since May 28, when he stabbed 17-year-old Surya Pratap Chauhan to death following an argument with the help of his associates on Bakrid before fleeing the scene. Three more accused, including Asad's father, have also been arrested in connection with the case.

I stopped taking cab to work. Delhi Metro changed both my budget and fitnessAs someone who had grown accustomed to relying on Uber and Rapido for my daily commute, switching back to the metro wasn't the easiest adjustment. But a few days in, it became clear that the change was worth it.

Bengal Cabinet expansion today, 35 new ministers to be sworn inWhen Suvendu Adhikari took oath as West Bengal's first BJP Chief Minister on May 9, only five other BJP MLAs — Agnimitra Paul, Nisith Pramanik, Dilip Ghosh, Ashok Kirtania and Kshudiram Tudu — were sworn in alongside him.

Voluntary sex work not illegal, police can't harass consenting adults: SCIn a landmark ruling aimed at addressing the concerns of victims of trafficking for commercial sexual exploitation, the Supreme Court has held that the consent of adult sex workers must be the primary consideration in decisions on rehabilitation, reintegration and placement in protective homes.

Anthropic overtakes OpenAI as the world's most valuable startup after latest funding roundAnthropic has now pulled ahead of OpenAI in the AI race, becoming the world's most valuable startup after a massive funding round.

sports.headline: RCB's Lakers era: A dynasty in the making, three-peat in sight IPL 2026: After an 18-year wait for their maiden title, RCB now have back-to-back IPL crowns. With a winning culture and long-term vision taking shape, are they building the IPL's next great dynasty, much like the LA Lakers in the NBA?

RCB's back-to-back titles built on foundations set by IPL mega-auction IPL 2026: RCB's back-to-back IPL titles weren't won on glamour, but engineered via mega-auction squad depth. By replacing individual reliance with quality backups, they built a bulletproof champion empire.

How father's tales of a helmetless Gavaskar forged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's grit On a historic IPL presentation night, 15-year-old MVP Vaibhav Sooryavanshi bypassed the usual corporate scripts to recall how his father's backyard stories of a helmetless Sunil Gavaskar forged his fearless approach to the game. He also made it clear that he wants to play Test cricket for India, sooner rather than later.

RCB rack up back-to-back IPL titles. Here's how CSK congratulated the champs IPL 2026 Final, RCB vs GT: Chennai Super Kings congratulated Royal Challengers Bengaluru with a special message after RCB secured their second successive IPL title with a five-wicket victory over Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.

MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma, Rajat Patidar: The IPL's most unlikely trilogy IPL 2026 final: He came as a replacement player, went unsold at auction, and once postponed his own wedding for a tournament that had seemingly forgotten him. Two IPL titles later, Rajat Patidar has joined the most exclusive club in Indian cricket, entirely on his own terms.

GT players evacuated as short circuit causes team bus breakdown after IPL final IPL final: Gujarat Titans players and support staff were evacuated from their team bus after a short circuit caused smoke to spread inside the vehicle following the IPL 2026 final in Ahmedabad. All members were safe, with an alternate bus arranged to take them to the hotel.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli sets the tone, then finishes off in style IPL final, Play of the Day: RCB beat Gujarat Titans to win their second Indian Premier League trophy. RCB joined a rare club on the back of Virat Kohli's terrific innings at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Rajat Patidar dedicates RCB's IPL title defence to Bengaluru stampede victims Rajat Patidar dedicated RCB's second successive IPL title to the 11 supporters who lost their lives in last year's Bengaluru stampede, linking the franchise's latest triumph to a season-long effort to honour the victims and their families.
After 19 years, Virat Kohli lives out his IPL dream 17 years of waiting and then RCB win back to back IPL titles. But on Sunday, May 31, Virat Kohli realised his long awaited dream.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi sweeps IPL awards, makes a scary statement at the end Vaibhav Sooryavanshi signed off a memorable IPL 2026 season with a clean sweep of the major individual awards, but the Rajasthan Royals teenager insisted there is still plenty of room for improvement.

Winning six, flying kiss: Kohli-Anushka moments go viral after RCB title win Virat Kohli's six sealed Royal Challengers Bengaluru's five-wicket win over Gujarat Titans before he blew a flying kiss towards Anushka Sharma in the stands. RCB won their second title on the trot.

RCB's champion's mindset is making Virat's job easier: Don't have to do it aloneVirat Kohli attributed Royal Challengers Bengaluru's second successive IPL crown to the squad's depth and collective strength, admitting he no longer feels solely responsible for the team's success.

18 years for one. 12 months for another. RCB are IPL champions, againEighteen years to win the first. Twelve months to win the second. Royal Challengers Bengaluru steamrolled Gujarat Titans in the IPL 2026 final to cement their place among cricket's most ruthless dynasties.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi enjoys IPL 2026 final with Jay Shah, sports new haircutVaibhav Sooryavanshi was spotted at the IPL 2026 final in Ahmedabad alongside Jay Shah. He arrived to collect major season awards after a record-breaking campaign and sported a new haircut as well.

French Open: Zverev through to quarters, teenage Jodar continues impressive runSecond seed Alexander Zverev moved into the French Open quarter-finals with a 7-6(3), 6-4, 6-1 win over lucky loser Jesper de Jong on Sunday, setting up a last-eight meeting with Spanish teenager Rafael Jodar.

How RCB's bowling plan ripped through Gujarat Titans in the IPL finalRoyal Challengers Bengaluru's pace attack made full use of a sluggish Ahmedabad surface, restricting Gujarat Titans to a below-par 155 for 8 in the IPL 2026 final at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.

Orange Cap at 15: Sooryavanshi eclipses Shubman Gill to finish IPL 2026 on topVaibhav Sooryavanshi finished IPL 2026 with the Orange Cap after Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan fell early in the final against RCB. The 15-year-old's 776-run season also made him the youngest winner of the accolade.

Be fair to GT: Gavaskar says IPL final should've been delayed after travel chaosSunil Gavaskar said the IPL 2026 final should have been moved to the reserve day after Gujarat Titans were hit by a travel delay. He argued the disruption left GT mentally and physically drained, making the scheduling unfair.

Ruturaj replaces Parag, named India A vice-captain for Sri Lanka tri-seriesRuturaj Gaikwad has replaced the injured Riyan Parag in the India A squad for the Sri Lanka tri-series and will serve as vice-captain. The call gives Gaikwad a chance to reset after a lean IPL while Parag moves to the Centre of Excellence for rehabilitation.

Satwik-Chirag script history, first Indian duo to win Singapore Open titleSingapore Opne 2026: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty ended their two-year title drought by winning the Singapore Open men's doubles crown. The Indian pair defeated Indonesia's Fajar Alfian and Muhammad Fikri 18-21, 21-17, 21-16 in a thrilling final to make history as the first Indian men's doubles team to win the event.

Former champion Iga Swiatek knocked out of Roland Garros in straight-set shockerRoland Garros 2026: Marta Kostyuk ruined Iga Swiatek's 25th birthday with a shocking 7-5, 6-1 upset at the French Open, guaranteeing a brand-new champion in Paris after a chaotic week of top-tier exits.

RCB vs GT Highlights: Virat Kohli guides RCB to 2nd IPL title in AhmedabadIPL Final 2026, Bengaluru (RCB) vs Gujarat (GT) Highlights: Virat Kohli smashed the fastest fifty of his IPL career as Royal Challengers Bengaluru chased down a target of 156 to defeat Gujarat Titans in the IPL 2026 final in Ahmedabad on Sunday. Despite a brief wobble triggered by Rashid Khan's double strike, RCB held their nerve to seal their second successive IPL title and become only the third franchise after Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians to win back-to-back championships.

Sachin Tendulkar shares the ultimate wishlist to reshape modern IPL cricketSachin Tendulkar has called for the IPL's Impact Player rule to be scrapped, saying it unfairly favours batters. He also proposed a bowling powerplay and a five-over quota for one bowler.

Singapore Open Final Highlights: SatChi are champions, win their first title in two yearsSingapore Open Final Highlights: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty have beaten Indonesia's Fajar Alfian and Muhammad Fikri in the Singapore Open men's doubles final, 18-21, 21-17, 21-16, to win their first title in two years.

An IPL trophy could be Shubman GIll's T20I ticket. Could beIPL 2026: Three IPL seasons, nearly 2,000 runs, and two World Cup snubs. He might win an IPL title as captain on Sunday. Shubman Gill has made the loudest possible case for a T20I recall. Whether India's selectors are listening is an entirely different question.

PM Modi on Mann ki Baat talks track rivalry with Gurindervir Singh and Animesh KujurPM Modi praised Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur for their record-breaking sprint feats, highlighting how the close friends push each other on the track while helping Indian athletics grow.

Sree Charani backs India to bounce back ahead of T20 World Cup after ENG lossWomen's T20 World Cup: Sree Charani has backed India to respond in Taunton after England's 26-run win in Bristol levelled the Women's T20I series. Her three wickets and calm appraisal underline how closely India are weighing form, temperament and bowling balance before the World Cup.

The grace of lift: Pooja Singh and the art of the high jumpPooja Singh's historic 1.93m high jump to win Asian U20 gold did more than shatter India's senior national record. Her journey from practicing on village rice sacks to mastering visual grace on the world stage redefines the beauty of flight.

Luis Enrique eyes more Champions League crowns: 'I can do four' after PSG winPSG beat Arsenal on penalties in Budapest to retain the Champions League under Luis Enrique. The win sealed back-to-back European titles and drew praise from Mikel Arteta and Nasser Al-Khelaifi.

Can Virat Kohli strike gold? Rashid Khan a big concern: Key stats for IPL 2026 finalIPL 2026, RCB vs GT: Rajat Patidar's Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans have all to play for when they lock horns in the grand finale on Sunday, May 31 at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Sachin Tendulkar's timeless advice for rising star Sooryavanshi after defeat vs GTIPL 2026: Sachin Tendulkar has urged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to stay true to his natural game amid mounting India talk. The advice captures both the scale of the 15-year-old's rise and the need to shield him from premature pressure.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
